calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Confirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
Off Calendar Mar-13-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Mak for Judy Chan (Petitiner/Trustee).

Ms. Mak stated that the accounts were opened after execution of the general assignment, that the accounts were generated from assets that were included in the general assignment, that she filed a supplement on December 7th and that the will provision directs that all assets upon his (the Husband) death go into the revocable trust. Ms. Mak also asked the Court to grant her request to avoid delay to the beneficiaries and avoid additional legal fees, stated that the accounts are not in joint tenancy, that they just held in their names and that they are held jointly. The Court stated that when an account is held jointly the parties are co-owners, that the accounts go directly to the Wife and questions why this is needed. Ms. Mak stated that the accounts are savings and checking accounts, and that the Husband intended for all of his assets to be put into the trust. The Petition for Order Confirming Certain Assets are Trust Property is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
